name: Datashader (Big Data Visualization)

performance.notes: Rasterizes billions of points; uses Numba/Dask for acceleration; output is fixed-size image

agent_readiness.notes: Rasterization pipeline for visualizing huge datasets (millions to billions of points). Pipeline: project → aggregate → transform → shade. Works with pandas, Dask, cuDF (GPU), Xarray. Outputs PIL/numpy images or Bokeh/Matplotlib overlays. Supports points, lines, polygons, rasters. Colormaps, spreading, histogram equalization. Used for: geospatial data, financial tick data, network graphs, large scatter plots. Core HoloViz tool, integrates with hvPlot and Panel.

gotchas: API is pipeline-oriented — different mental model than traditional plotting
Output is a rasterized image, not interactive vector graphics
Requires Numba for full performance (JIT compilation)
best_when: Visualizing millions to billions of data points
Financial tick data or time series with dense overplotting
Embedding large-scale scatter plots in dashboards
avoid_when: Small datasets (< 100k points) — standard matplotlib/plotly is better
Interactive drill-down per data point (rasterized output loses per-point info)
summary: Datashader rasterizes huge datasets (millions to billions of points) into pixel-perfect visualizations by aggregating data before rendering. Uses a pipeline: project → aggregate → transform → shade. Works with pandas, Dask, cuDF. GPU-accelerated via RAPIDS. Integrates with hvPlot and Panel for interactive dashboards. Essential for geospatial data, financial time series, and any visualization with overplotting problems at scale.
